Arctium lappa, also known as great burdock, is a biennial thistle-like plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its large, edible leaves and distinctive purple flowers. Arctium lappa has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Arctium lappa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Arctium lappa is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine as a treatment for various health conditions.
